name: ask-chatgpt-dev
owns: /home/abhmul/dev/ask-chatgpt (the ask-chatgpt code repo, working branch 'main' or feature branches off it)
src/ask_chatgpt/  (the library — to be ARCHIVED and REWRITTEN per operator directive)

ground_truth.acceptance_notes: Mock suite is the default tier; real_site tests are deselected by default (pyproject addopts '-m not real_site') AND additionally gated on ASK_CHATGPT_REAL=1. Re-derive every verdict from inspected command output and produced artifact files, NEVER from exit codes or log lines alone (a wrapper script's exit 0 masked a pytest exit 1 during setup). 'uv run' uses the PROJECT venv (.venv), which is entirely separate from the uv TOOL install.
ground_truth.verdict_notes: VERIFICATION.md currently verifies the OLD library and becomes stale on rewrite; re-issue it honestly for the rewritten library, with a falsifiability + prompt-quality lens, not green-exit alone.
ground_truth.real_site: operator-attended CDP runs only; not CI/cron/unattended

installed_tool.fact: ask-chatgpt v0.0.1 is installed via 'uv tool install' as an ISOLATED COPY built from the git branch 'stable' (file:///home/abhmul/dev/ask-chatgpt?rev=stable). Entry point /home/abhmul/.local/bin/ask-chatgpt runs the tool's own frozen venv at /home/abhmul/.local/share/uv/tools/ask-chatgpt. site-packages/ask_chatgpt is a real copied dir (NOT editable, NO .pth/direct_url editable marker). 'stable' currently = 779eb40 (4 commits behind main@7483806).
installed_tool.another_agent: A separate agent is concurrently USING the installed tool. Do not disrupt it.
installed_tool.isolation_rule: Editing the working tree CANNOT affect the running tool. The tool only changes if (a) the 'stable' branch is moved/committed-to, or (b) someone runs uv tool install/upgrade/reinstall. THEREFORE: NEVER commit to or move 'stable'; NEVER run 'uv tool install/upgrade/reinstall' against this repo during development. Do all rework on 'main' or feature branches.

shared_resources.chatgpt_account: operator-owned single account. Human-paced; NO programmatic spamming. NO hard per-run message cap (the old 'max N messages' was a retracted fiction). Real-site automation is opt-in/attended; login is NEVER automated; any Cloudflare/login challenge -> STOP, log HUMAN-ACTION-NEEDED, poll read-only.
shared_resources.operator_cdp_browser: Real-site legs attach over CDP to the operator's signed-in Chromium at http://127.0.0.1:9222 (profile 'agent' = dir 'Profile 1'). Playwright-LAUNCHED browsers are Cloudflare-blocked, hence CDP attach. The operator (and/or another agent) may be using this browser concurrently: inspect ONLY tabs the tool itself opens; NEVER read/touch operator tabs (leak risk); NEVER quit the browser (detach only). Preflight curl 127.0.0.1:9222/json/version before any real leg; concurrency modest (~3-way).
shared_resources.agent_python_venv: ~/.local/share/agent-python/.venv is READ-ONLY shared infra for claude/pi/codex agents (see ~/Documents/vaults/agent-vault/agent-python/README). This repo's deps live in its own uv PROJECT venv, separate from both the agent venv and the uv tool install.

vcs_layout: single-team => repo-only (D12): plain checkout, NO mesh, NO worktrees, NO team-system repo. Team identity/state lives in this repo under team/. If a SECOND team is later created (operator-reserved), migrate to the bare-clone + per-team-worktree layout via team-system bootstrap.
